0.q: VfSBP4 and VfSBP17 influence plant stress resistance by reducing immune-regulatory responses and disrupting reactive oxygen species (ROS) homeostasis, thereby promoting pathogen infection.

1.q: VfSBP4 and VfSBP17 influence plant stress resistance by reducing immune-regulatory responses and disrupting reactive oxygen species (ROS) homeostasis, thereby promoting pathogen infection.

2.q: Under drought and salt stress conditions, VfSBP4 and VfSBP17 overexpression increased stomatal aperture and water loss, exacerbating leaf wilting.

3.q: Under drought and salt stress conditions, VfSBP4 and VfSBP17 overexpression increased stomatal aperture and water loss, exacerbating leaf wilting.

4.q: VfSBP10 and VfSBP12 enhance resistance by inhibiting pathogen infection and maintaining ROS balance.
